_comment: The CLIENT tools BrailleBuddy uses. Recreate BOTH in the ElevenLabs dashboard (Agent → Tools → Add tool → type: Client). Names must match apps/web/components/Conversation.tsx EXACTLY (case-sensitive). Pass the LETTER/WORD itself; the web app (apps/web/lib/braille.ts) looks up the dot patterns. Also add the four dynamic variables (user_name, level, lesson_title, known_letters) under Agent → the prompt uses them as {{var}} and the web passes them at session start.

tools.name: render_braille
tools.description: Raise the pins on the single braille cell to show ONE character, then drop them after the given time. Use for teaching single letters. Pass the letter itself (not a dot code) — the device looks up the pattern.

parameters.description: The single character to show, e.g. "C". If more than one character is sent, only the first is used.
How long the character stays raised before the pins drop. Use about 3 for teaching.

tools.name: render_word
tools.description: Step a whole WORD across the single cell, one letter at a time, resetting between letters. The on-screen word strip highlights each letter as it shows. Use for reading words and short phrases. Pass the word itself.

parameters.description: The word to spell out on the cell, e.g. "cat".
How long each letter stays raised, in seconds. Use about 1.5 for reading practice.

dynamic_variables._comment: Passed by the web app at session start (see Conversation.tsx). Reference in the prompt as {{name}}.
dynamic_variables.known_letters: comma-separated letters the learner already knows
